perturbation.original_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Information, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Information, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered by Holdings and its Representatives to Big Sky.
perturbation.changed_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Information, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Information, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered by Holdings and its Representatives to Big Sky. However, any analyses or reports generated by Holdings using the Information need not be returned and may be retained indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The new sentence allows Holdings to retain indefinitely any analyses or reports, which are likely to contain the original information. It contradicts the first sentence which requires that at the conclusion of their review of the Information, all copies of the Information will be delivered back. This is contradictory because the initial agreement was to return ALL copies of the Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Information, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Information, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered by Holdings and its Representatives to Big Sky.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that 'all copies of the Information, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered...'. The changed text adds an exception that 'any analyses or reports generated by Holdings using the Information need not be returned and may be retained indefinitely'. This directly contradicts the original requirement to return all copies, as analyses and reports are very likely to contain copies/representations of the initial information.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that it has been informed that the other party is a publicly-traded corporation and that the Information and the Transaction must be secured from any use or dissemination until such time as proper disclosure can be provided to shareholders and the securities markets in order to protect the interests of shareholders of each party and so that the rules and statutes pertaining to information about and trading in publicly held securities can be followed.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that it has been informed that the other party is a publicly-traded corporation and that the Information and the Transaction must be secured from any use or dissemination until such time as proper disclosure can be provided to shareholders and the securities markets in order to protect the interests of shareholders of each party and so that the rules and statutes pertaining to information about and trading in publicly held securities can be followed. Notwithstanding the above, either party may disclose the information to its financial advisors without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause contradicts the requirement for securing the information. The new statement allows disclosure to financial advisors without notice, creating uncertainty about the confidentiality obligation. This change now create an in-text contradiction with the start of the sentence, saying that transaction must be secured and later allowing to be disclosed to others
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that it has been informed that the other party is a publicly-traded corporation and that the Information and the Transaction must be secured from any use or dissemination until such time as proper disclosure can be provided to shareholders and the securities markets in order to protect the interests of shareholders of each party and so that the rules and statutes pertaining to information about and trading in publicly held securities can be followed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows disclosure to financial advisors without prior notice, which directly contradicts the original statement that 'the Information and the Transaction must be secured from any use or dissemination.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the requirement to secure the information is undermined by the allowance of disclosure, making the confidentiality obligation uncertain. The contradicted text is identical to the Original text making the contradiction clear.

perturbation.original_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 60 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other, without the express written consent of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 60 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other, without the express written consent of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party. This restriction does not apply to transactions executed pursuant to pre-existing trading plans established in compliance with Rule 10b5-1 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence creates an exception for trades under Rule 10b5-1 plans. While such plans aim to avoid insider trading, allowing these trades without consent contradicts the initial, broad prohibition on stock transactions. This is a contradictory statement, due to the initial statement that says that there should not be any engagement on any transactions regarding the common stock
perturbation.contradicted_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 60 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other, without the express written consent of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes a broad restriction on stock transactions, requiring express written consent. The changed text introduces an exception for transactions under Rule 10b5-1 plans. The contradicted text reiterates the initial broad restriction. This exception directly conflicts with the original blanket prohibition, creating an in-text contradiction because transactions can now occur without express written consent, thereby contradicting the original text's terms, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by it, the other would be irreparably and immediately harmed and could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, it is agreed that, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at law or in equity, each party shall be entitled to an injunction of injunctions (without the posting of any bond and without proof of actual damages) to prevent breaches or threatened breaches of this Agreement and/or to compel specific performance of this Agreement, and that neither party nor any of its Representatives will oppose the granting of such relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by it, the other would be irreparably and immediately harmed and could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, it is agreed that, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at law or in equity, each party shall be entitled to an injunction of injunctions (without the posting of any bond and without proof of actual damages) to prevent breaches or threatened breaches of this Agreement and/or to compel specific performance of this Agreement, and that neither party nor any of its Representatives will oppose the granting of such relief. However, any claim for injunctive relief must be preceded by a thirty-day written notice to the breaching party, during which time the breaching party may cure the breach.
perturbation.explanation: This new stipulation contradicts the 'immediately harmed' premise of the original clause. The thirty-day cure period undermines the idea of immediate and irreparable harm. This new statement is in text contradiction with the initial statement that any breach will cause irreparable harm, meaning that the cure period of 30 days is contradicting that statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by it, the other would be irreparably and immediately harmed and could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, it is agreed that, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at law or in equity, each party shall be entitled to an injunction of injunctions (without the posting of any bond and without proof of actual damages) to prevent breaches or threatened breaches of this Agreement and/or to compel specific performance of this Agreement, and that neither party nor any of its Representatives will oppose the granting of such relief.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a thirty-day written notice and cure period before injunctive relief can be sought. This directly contradicts the original statement, which is also the contradicted text, stating that any breach would cause 'irreparable and immediately harmed'. The cure period implies that the harm is not necessarily immediate or irreparable, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the perturbation explanation. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the addition of the cure period conflicts with the initial assertion of immediate and irreparable harm.
